title: Han River Paper Moon
prompt: Create an original Korean indie pop track at 96 BPM in E major, centered on a soft urban groove with brushed kick, dry rim snare, light shaker, finger snaps, and a round melodic bass that moves gently under the vocal. The vocal should be tender, close-mic, and youthful, with intimate verse phrasing and a chorus that opens into small harmony stacks. Start with guitar and room tone, move into a sparse verse, lift through a pre-chorus with keys and snaps, then bloom into a chorus with fuller drums and wide pads. Add a bridge with stripped guitar, bass, and vocal layers before the final chorus, then fade on bells and guitar. Mix it soft, clear, and polished with warm mids and a gentle indie master.

Use tight kick, dry snare, soft hats, finger snaps, and a rounded synth bass that gives the chorus a subtle dance lift. Main instruments should include felt piano, accordion fragments, pizzicato-like strings, glassy synth arps, and muted guitar accents. The vocal should be poised, intimate, and dramatic, with rhythmically spoken verse moments and a chorus that opens into clear melodic lines. Start with piano, snaps, and a filtered synth arp, move into a lean verse, build a pre-chorus with string tension, then land on a chorus with full drums and bass. Add a breakdown with accordion fragments and vocal echoes before the final chorus. Mix it glossy, stylish, and balanced with clean vocal detail.
exclude: specific artist references, copyrighted melodies, copied chanson hooks, cheesy dance remixes, aggressive techno bass, shrill strings, overprocessed vocals, muddy synth pads

title: Café Neon Waltz
prompt: Create an original French chanson-pop song at 78 BPM in A minor, using a slow 3/4 pulse with brushed snare, soft kick, light tambourine, and an upright-style bass that sways gently. Use accordion, piano, nylon guitar, soft string pads, and delicate bell tones to create a romantic night café texture with modern clarity. The vocal should be tender, expressive, and close, carrying story-like verses and a chorus that grows through harmonies and subtle strings. Begin with solo piano and accordion, enter a sparse verse, build the pre-chorus with guitar and bass, then bloom into a chorus with fuller percussion and layered vocals. Add a bridge with only piano, bells, and voice before the final chorus returns softly. Mix it warm, intimate, and organic, with natural room tone and a gentle master.
exclude: specific artist references, copyrighted melodies, traditional standard quotations, accordion parody, rock power chords, heavy 808s, excessive vibrato, washed-out reverb

title: Bistro Synth Bouquet
prompt: Create an original French chanson-pop hybrid at 116 BPM in E-flat major, turning classic melodic storytelling into a bright modern pop groove. Build the rhythm with crisp kick, snare, claps, brushed hats, and a warm bass that walks elegantly while still locking to the beat. Main instruments should include piano stabs, accordion pads, clean guitar, subtle brass-like synths, and sparkling arpeggios that lift the chorus. The vocal should be witty, graceful, and melodic, with quick narrative verses and a chorus supported by polished backing harmonies. Structure it with a piano-and-clap intro, verse with light accordion, pre-chorus adding bass climb and arps, chorus with full drums and brass color, bridge with stripped snaps and guitar, final chorus, and a neat piano tag. Mix it bright, charming, and radio-ready.
exclude: specific artist references, copyrighted melodies, copied musical-theater motifs, circus feel, hardstyle kick, trap hi-hats, harsh brass synths, cluttered accordion

title: Vespa Mirrorball
prompt: Create an original Italian disco pop track at 120 BPM in A minor, with a sleek four-on-the-floor groove, tight kick, crisp clap-snare, open hats, shaker, and a warm octave bass line that drives the chorus without sounding heavy. Use bright piano stabs, clean funk guitar, analog string machine, brass-synth accents, and shimmering arpeggios for a glossy Mediterranean night feel. The vocal should be smooth, passionate, and stylish, with rhythmic verses and a wide chorus supported by stacked harmonies. Start with bass and arpeggio intro, enter a verse with lean drums, build a pre-chorus with strings and claps, then release into a chorus with full disco rhythm. Add a bridge with filtered drums, guitar chops, and synth strings before the final chorus and a bright instrumental tag. Mix it punchy, shiny, and dance-floor ready with controlled lows.
exclude: specific artist references, copyrighted melodies, copied disco hooks, harsh EDM supersaws, trap 808 slides, rock guitar solos, excessive cowbell, clipped master

title: Amalfi Chrome Dance
prompt: Create an original Italian disco pop song at 124 BPM in C major, built around a sunny dance groove with steady kick, tight snare-clap, bright hats, conga touches, and a rounded bass that bounces through elegant chord changes. title: Tiramisu Laser Kiss
